name: Uber Direct API
what_it_does: White-label on-demand delivery API that dispatches Uber's global courier network for same-day last-mile delivery without consumer-facing Uber branding.
use_cases: Dispatching on-demand couriers for retail, grocery, or restaurant businesses
Building branded delivery experiences with global coverage in 6,000+ cities
Getting delivery quotes and ETAs before committing to a shipment
Real-time delivery tracking via webhooks and polling
Integrating last-mile delivery into existing order management systems
not_for: Businesses requiring SLA guarantees below 30-minute windows
best_when: You need global last-mile delivery coverage across multiple countries with a single API integration and white-label branding.
avoid_when: You need US-only delivery at the lowest per-delivery cost, or need to avoid OAuth complexity in your agent's auth flow.

auth.notes: OAuth 2.0 client credentials flow for server-to-server. Scopes include eats.deliveries for delivery operations. Access tokens expire and must be refreshed.

pricing.paid_tiers: Per-delivery fee via enterprise agreement; sandbox available for testing
pricing.notes: Requires business partnership and contract. Per-delivery pricing negotiated based on volume. Sandbox environment is free for development.

performance.rate_limits: Not publicly documented; enforced per client_id

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Structured error responses with error codes and messages; some edge cases underdocumented
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Supports idempotency keys on delivery creation via request headers; not enforced on all endpoints
known_agent_gotchas: OAuth token refresh must be handled — access tokens expire and are not auto-renewed
Production access requires a signed partnership agreement; agents cannot self-onboard
Deliveries are financially live on creation — build confirmation and dry-run logic into agents
Coverage availability must be checked before dispatching or API returns 422
Webhook payload verification (HMAC) is mandatory for security
Rate limits not publicly documented — test carefully in staging before production

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced; OAuth 2.0 with scopes; HMAC webhook verification; GDPR compliance for EU operations
